title: Check 1A PASS: All stream references use RedisStreams constants
evidence: Grep of xaddWithLimit/xadd/xreadGroup/createConsumerGroup across all non-test .ts files in services/src/ and shared/core/src/. All 40+ xaddWithLimit callers use RedisStreamsClient.STREAMS.* or RedisStreams.* from @arbitrage/types. No hardcoded 'stream:*' literals found in any Redis operation call site. One log-only fallback 'stream:execution-requests' at services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:360 is cosmetic (not passed to Redis).
expected: All stream name references use RedisStreams constants from shared/types/src/events.ts
actual: All stream names use RedisStreams constants. Zero violations.
recommendation: No action needed. Stream constant discipline is excellent.

title: Test mock core.mock.ts missing 5 stream entries vs source of truth
evidence: shared/test-utils/src/mocks/core.mock.ts has 24 STREAMS entries; shared/types/src/events.ts has 29 entries. Missing: EXEC_REQUESTS_FAST, EXEC_REQUESTS_L2, EXEC_REQUESTS_PREMIUM, EXEC_REQUESTS_SOLANA, PRE_SIMULATED.
expected: Mock STREAMS object should mirror all entries from RedisStreams constant
actual: Mock is missing 5 stream entries added in Phase 2 (ADR-038) and async pipeline split
recommendation: Add the 5 missing stream entries to shared/test-utils/src/mocks/core.mock.ts to prevent test failures if tests reference these streams.

title: Check 1B PASS: All consumer groups match expected inventory
evidence: Found in non-test source: coordinator-group (coordinator.ts:399,1137; stream-health-monitor.ts:124), cross-chain-detector-group (detector.ts:333,346,353), execution-engine-group (fast-lane.consumer.ts:103, opportunity.consumer.ts:184), simulation-worker-group (engine.ts:769), mempool-detector-group (mempool-config.ts:78). Also: orderflow-pipeline, self-healing-manager, failover-${serviceName} (dynamic).
expected: coordinator-group, cross-chain-detector-group, execution-engine-group, simulation-worker-group, mempool-detector-group, orderflow-pipeline, self-healing-manager, failover-*
actual: All expected groups found. Zero unknown consumer groups in production source code.

title: Check 1C PASS: No raw xadd() without MAXLEN in production callers (streams.ts)
evidence: streams.ts changed file analysis: xadd() method at line 668 has a code path at line 727 without MAXLEN when options.maxLen is undefined. However, xaddWithLimit() at line 765 always resolves MAXLEN from STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS registry with SA-1C-004 DEFAULT_STREAM_MAX_LENGTH=5000 fallback for unregistered streams. Internal DLQ forwarding at line 951 uses xaddWithLimit(). All 40+ production callers in services/ and shared/ use xaddWithLimit(), not raw xadd(). The raw xadd() without MAXLEN is the internal low-level method; public API is xaddWithLimit().
expected: All xadd operations in production use MAXLEN to prevent unbounded stream growth
actual: Architecture is correct. xaddWithLimit() wraps xadd() with auto-MAXLEN lookup. No production code bypasses the wrapper.
recommendation: No action needed. Low-level xadd() is intentionally available for the wrapper to call.

title: Check 1D PASS: xreadgroup properly paired with xack in streams.ts
evidence: streams.ts changed file analysis: xreadgroup() at line 887 returns StreamMessage[] for caller to process. xack() at line 981 is separate method called by StreamConsumer after processing. HMAC-rejected messages at line 936 are ACKed inline via client.xack() to prevent PEL growth (OP-9 FIX). batchXack() at line 1002 provides pipelined ACK for larger batches. StreamConsumer class (stream-consumer.ts:284) manages the full read-process-ack lifecycle.
expected: xreadgroup and xack calls are properly paired in the consumer lifecycle
actual: Architecture is correct: read -> process -> ack flow managed by StreamConsumer class. No xreadgroup without corresponding xack path.
recommendation: No action needed. ACK lifecycle is properly managed.

title: EXECUTION_ENGINE_HOST now documented in .env.example
evidence: .env.example:69 contains '# EXECUTION_ENGINE_HOST=localhost'. Previously flagged as HIGH in prior session. Verified with grep: line 69 of .env.example shows the commented entry.
actual: EXECUTION_ENGINE_HOST is documented as a commented entry at line 69 of .env.example
recommendation: No further action needed. Prior finding SA-1E-001 HIGH is resolved.

title: Deprecated LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT shim still referenced in streams.ts
evidence: shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:1685 and :1751 check process.env.LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT and emit deprecation warning to rename to STREAM_LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT. The code correctly reads STREAM_LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT for actual behavior. This is a migration shim with TODO(breaking-change) at line 1683.
expected: Deprecated env vars eventually removed from source
actual: LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT deprecation shim present in 2 locations. Not a bug — warning logging is correct behavior. Canonical STREAM_LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT is documented at .env.example:660.
recommendation: Track for removal in next breaking-change cycle. No .env.example change needed.

title: Check 1E Summary: 0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH (SA-1E-001 RESOLVED), 1 LOW
evidence: Scanned all non-test .ts files under services/src/ and shared/*/src/. Found 267+ unique process.env.* references. Compared against .env.example entries (both commented # and uncommented). Excluded platform vars (NODE_ENV, PORT, CI, JEST_WORKER_ID, HOME, PATH, HOSTNAME, FLY_APP_NAME, RENDER_SERVICE_NAME, RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT, KOYEB_SERVICE_NAME, OCI_RESOURCE_PRINCIPAL_VERSION). Excluded JSDoc-only vars (TTL_MS, SUCCESS_RATE, FEE_MULTIPLIER in env-parsing.ts examples). Result: 0 CRITICAL (no undocumented KEY/SECRET/TOKEN/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/PRIVATE vars), 0 HIGH (EXECUTION_ENGINE_HOST now documented), 1 LOW (LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT deprecated alias).
recommendation: No urgent action. Schedule LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT removal in next major version.
